title: palicourea sucllii
intro: Palicourea sucllii, also known as the "yellow senna", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Palicourea sucllii include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of malaria. The plant's herbal preparations typically involve drying and processing its leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or other medicinal remedies.
